channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Stephen Miller sidesteps question on US troops in Venezuela as tensions rise
item.description: White House deputy chief of staff Stephen Miller on Friday dodged a question from reporters about the potential for ground troops on Venezuelan soil, as tensions escalate amid the Trump administration's war on drugs in the Caribbean.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Ivory Coast votes as Ouattara seeks a fourth term with key rivals sidelined
item.description: ABIDJAN, Ivory Coast (AP) — Voters in Ivory Coast cast ballots Saturday to elect a new leader as longtime President Alassane Ouattara seeks a fourth term, while his key contenders were barred from the race.
item.dc:creator: OPE ADETAYO and CHINEDU ASADU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump rips Canada's 'crooked' anti-tariffs ad as possibly AI
item.description: President Trump took another swing at Canada late Friday, calling a controversial anti-tariff ad featuring excerpts from a former President Reagan speech, "crooked" and "dishonest." He also suggested that the video was "possibly" generated by artificial intelligence (AI).
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Malaysia warns ASEAN neutrality eroding as global powers compete
item.description: KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia (AP) — Malaysian foreign minister warned his counterparts from Southeast Asia that the space for neutrality is shrinking in a region increasingly shaped by big power rivalry, as they mark U.S. President Donald Trump’s first trip to Asia since returning to the White House.
item.dc:creator: EILEEN NG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Grandmothers in Colombia get the quinceañera they never had
item.description: At age 72, Rosalba Casas finally celebrated her 15th birthday, donning a flowing pink dress and a tiara for the quinceañera she dreamed of as a teenager.
item.dc:creator: Astrid Suárez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russia faces a shrinking and aging population and tries restrictive laws to combat it
item.description: Russia has grappled with a shrinking and aging population for decades, with President Vladimir Putin viewing it as a threat to national security.
item.dc:creator: Katie Marie Davies, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Europe fights to avoid being sidelined by Trump’s world-shaping plans
item.description: Donald Trump has seized a central role on the global stage, leaving European allies on the sidelines.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa stationary in the Caribbean as 4 deaths reported and huge rains expected
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Tropical Storm Melissa was nearly stationary in the central Caribbean on Friday as forecasters warned it could strengthen and swipe Jamaica as a powerful hurricane and dump a staggering amount of rain — up to 35 inches (89 centimeters) — on southwest Haiti, where they wa
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, JOHN MYERS JR. and EVENS SANON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Japan's new leader faces diplomatic gauntlet with Trump, China and regional summits
item.description: Japan's new Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi faces significant foreign policy challenges just days after taking office.
item.dc:creator: Ken Moritsugu And Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukraine’s Zelenskyy urges US to broaden Russian oil sanctions and seeks long-range missiles
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Friday urged the United States to expand sanctions on Russian oil from two companies to the whole sector, and appealed for long-range missiles to hit back at Russia.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Houthi rebels detain 2 more UN staffers in Yemen as the world body reevaluates its operations
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Houthi rebels detained two additional United Nations staff members and raided the homes of several others in Yemen over the last 48 hours, the latest in a series of events now forcing the world body to reassess how it operates in the war-torn country.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and AHMED AL-HAJ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Frantic manhunt launched after asylum seeker who sexually assaulted teen accidentally freed from prison
item.description: An Ethiopian asylum seeker who sexually assaulted 14-year-old girl was accidentally released from a U.K. prison instead of being deported, sparking an urgent manhunt.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reagan ad that infuriated Trump set to run during World Series
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford said Friday the anti-tariffs advertisement featuring former President Reagan, which angered President Trump, will air during the World Series before being paused Monday. The ad led to Trump calling off trade negotiations between Canada and the United States.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Rubio tours US-led center in Israel overseeing the ceasefire in Gaza
item.description: KIRYAT GAT, Israel (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Friday toured a U.S.-led center in Israel overseeing the Gaza ceasefire, as the Trump administration worked to set up an international security force in the territory and shore up the tenuous truce between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Afghanistan and Pakistan head to Turkey for second round of crisis talks
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Afghanistan and Pakistan are heading to Turkey for a second round of crisis talks, officials said Friday, after recent fighting between them killed dozens of people and injured hundreds more.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon sends largest aircraft carrier to Caribbean amid boat strikes
item.description: The Pentagon is sending the world’s largest aircraft carrier, USS Gerald R. Ford, and its carrier air wing to the Caribbean as the Trump administration conducts strikes against boats it says are smuggling illegal drugs in the region.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: US deploys Ford carrier strike group to combat narco-terror in Western Hemisphere
item.description: The Gerald R. Ford Carrier Strike Group is being deployed to continue targeting suspected drug smuggling boats in the Caribbean, the Pentagon said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ontario premier says he'll pull ad that upset Trump so trade talks between Canada and US can resume
item.description: The leader of Canada’s most populous province says he’ll pull the anti-tariff ad that prompted U.S. President Donald Trump to end trade talks with Canada.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says declaration of war not needed for strikes on alleged cartel boats
item.description: President Trump on Thursday dismissed the need to ask Congress for a declaration of war as the U.S. military continuously launches strikes on boats in the Caribbean Sea and Pacific Ocean. “I don’t think we’re going to necessarily ask for a declaration of war,” Trump told reporters.
item.dc:creator: Max Rego, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine's GOP allies look to seize Trump's momentum versus Putin
item.description: Senators are hoping to seize the momentum as President Trump goes on the offensive against Russian President Vladimir Putin, though Republicans remain cautious about getting ahead of the commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Putin deplores US sanctions as 'unfriendly' while EU joins in heaping restrictions on Russia
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The European Union on Thursday heaped more economic sanctions on Russia, adding to U.S. President Donald Trump’s new punitive measures the previous day against the Russian oil industry.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hundreds of thousands of Hungarians attend rival rallies in Budapest as Orbán faces election test
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of Hungarians filled the streets of Budapest on Thursday in competing demonstrations as supporters of the country’s two main political movements staged mutual shows of strength before next spring’s national election.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin says Russia won't bow to the US
item.description: President Vladimir Putin cautioned that it would deliver an “overwhelming” response to any military strikes deep inside Russia.
item.dc:creator: By Ksenia Orlova and Maxim Rodionov, NewsNation

item.title: EU leaders seek role in Gaza at summit focused on Ukraine and Russia
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union leaders are seeking a more active role in Gaza and the occupied West Bank after being sidelined from the U.S.-brokered ceasefire between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: SAM McNEIL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: North Korea fires missiles as Trump prepares for South Korea visit
item.description: SEOUL, Oct 22 (Reuters) - North Korea fired what appeared to be multiple short-range ballistic missiles on Wednesday, South Korea's military said, a week before a key Asia-Pacific leaders' meeting in South Korea.
item.dc:creator: Jack Kim and Heejin Kim, NewsNation

item.title: Massive Russian attack on Ukraine kills at least 6, officials say
item.description: Ukraine’s energy minister said a “massive combined overnight attack” on Ukraine’s energy infrastructure was still underway early Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Paris prosecutor says stolen Louvre jewels worth an estimated $102 million
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The Paris prosecutor said Tuesday that crown jewels stolen in a dramatic weekend Louvre heist were worth an estimated 88 million euros ($102 million), but that the monetary estimate doesn’t include their historical value to France.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Burqa ban: Portugal may make it illegal to cover face in public
item.description: If signed into law, Portugal would join Austria, France, Belgium and the Netherlands, who have full or partial bans on face and head coverings.
item.dc:creator: Susie Pinto, NewsNation

item.title: Colombia recalls US ambassador after Trump's tariff threat, drug remarks
item.description: Trump also called Colombian's leftist President Gustavo Petro an "illegal drug leader" on Sunday, which Petro's government described as offensive.
item.dc:creator: Luis Jaime Acosta and Julia Symmes Cobb / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Pope meets with global group of clergy abuse survivors to talk zero tolerance
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Leo XIV met Monday for the first time with an organization of clergy abuse survivors and advocates, who said he agreed to maintain a permanent dialogue as they press for a zero-tolerance policy for abuse in the Catholic Church.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: China accuses US of cyberattack on national time center
item.description: China said any damage to related facilities could have disrupted network communications, financial systems and power supply.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
